note: Canonical machine-readable twin of the Claims Ledger. Verified rows carry verified_against='a1c9f8b' (the commit the enforcement layer landed on). Removed rows carry eq='EQ-D' (no evidence) since the markdown does not grade struck rows on the EQ axis. Evidence Code:/Test: paths are normalized to repo-root-relative form so they resolve at HEAD; the ledger abbreviates some of them.

claims.claim: Deterministic, float-free canonical serialization envelope (canon, payload_digest, EnvelopeChain); Unicode NFC normalization was removed so the Python path is byte-identical to the TS T0 path (Codex fix 1).
claims.fails_if: canon() reintroduces NFC/unicodedata, accepts a raw float without raising, or payload_digest is non-deterministic across two runs of the same payload.

claims.claim: The TS and Python paths produce identical digests; a decomposed-Unicode vector proves neither path normalizes (digest a2b964dcb6a519031c512c9714172760861f305c9b7050917e4378d8449854fd).
claims.fails_if: Equivalent payloads serialized on two supported runtimes (TS canonicalizeJCS, Python canon) produce different SHA-256 digests for any shared vector, including the decomposed-Unicode one.

claims.claim: Hash-chained, tamper-evident envelope: prev_hash links envelope N->N-1, seq is a monotonic per-process counter (never a timestamp), genesis prev_hash = GENESIS (64 zeros).
claims.fails_if: Genesis prev_hash != 64 zeros, seq does not increment by exactly 1 per emit, or altering one envelope's body leaves a later envelope's prev_hash unchanged.

claims.claim: Dual-emit on the live path: legacy hashes stay byte-identical and the canonical envelope is additive; the collaboration request_digest covers the full field set (objective, mode, live, generation, autonomous, max_agents, memory_context) (Codex fix 2).
claims.fails_if: The collaboration request_digest omits any of the seven input fields (source-pinned: the test extracts bridge.py's hashed key set and asserts it equals the 7-field set), or flipping any one execution-affecting field leaves request_digest unchanged. The additive-dual-emit half (adding the envelope changes no pre-existing legacy hash byte) remains confirmable by inspection at bridge.py:576-600.

claims.claim: Frozen-file hash integrity gate: three constitutional files are pinned by SHA-256 and the gate exits non-zero on mismatch. Ran it at db9d8a0 -> exit 0.
claims.fails_if: Mutating one byte of gate.py/dna.py/router.py does not make verify-hashes.mjs exit non-zero, or its pins diverge from the CLAUDE.md Constitutional Files table.

claims.claim: Cross-language independent replay (executor != verifier): a Python emitter, a Node re-chainer, and a Rust re-chainer independently re-derive the chain.
claims.fails_if: Running verifiable/cross_language/verify.sh yields a non-zero exit, or the Node and Rust re-chainers derive a chain hash differing from the Python emitter's for the shared stages.json.

claims.claim: Server-side provider gate + model provenance in the audit record: a client cannot force a paid backend via the request body, and the model actually used is reported back to callers (Codex fixes 5/7).
claims.fails_if: A request with provider:"openai" while CHAT_ENABLE_OPENAI!="true" reaches the paid backend instead of falling back to dashscope, or the returned model is not the one actually invoked. Held at EQ-B: the client-side provenance half (the router records the edge function's reported data.model, not the caller's req.model) is now mechanically pinned by inference-router.test.ts, but the server-side provider gate lives in the Deno chat function and cannot be unit-tested without a Deno runtime — it remains inspection-only at chat/index.ts:45-57.

claims.claim: Determinism test discipline: determinism tests run the subject >=3 times and assert byte-identical output.
claims.fails_if: The determinism tests run the subject fewer than 3 times, or accept a run whose output differs byte-for-byte from the first.

claims.claim: The AEGIS swarm runtime default model was claude-fable-5 from 2026-06-10 (commit 4747755, PR #148) to 2026-06-23 (commit 8305ec6, PR #172); it was never the frontend code default.
evidence: Code: packages/shared/lib/inference-router.ts:96 (frontend default was and is claude-haiku-4-5-20251001)
Repro: git log --all -S"claude-fable-5" --oneline; git show 4747755; git show 8305ec6
claims.fails_if: Pickaxe over reachable history (git log --all -S"claude-fable-5") stops listing 4747755/8305ec6, or those commits' diffs stop containing the quoted default flips (claude-sonnet-4-6 -> claude-fable-5 at 4747755; claude-fable-5 -> claude-opus-4-8 at 8305ec6).

claims.claim: Cross-language primitive hashing parity: the φ constant's IEEE-754 f64 big-endian bytes followed by SHA-256 produce byte-identical digests in Node, Python, and Rust (the Rust leg reproduced with the same sha2 0.10 crate the aegis-cl-psi gate modules use). Computed form (√5−1)/2 → BE 3fe3c6ef372fe950, SHA-256 b04b39a97f3554cde014326fd4c4864cc28b56bc66e17ff76e268ceac0e5ee18; literal form 0.6180339887498948 → BE 3fe3c6ef372fe94f, SHA-256 82298488842f4c6b76306bdadc4f28219d56248da4a44c299e550a4c2a858bf5. This is primitive-level (f64 BE + SHA-256) parity, NOT full tri-language envelope/structure replay (see CLM-207). The same instrumented proof surfaces a determinism-hygiene finding: the computed and literal φ representations differ by exactly one ULP (…e950 vs …e94f).
evidence: Proof: docs/proofs/cross-platform-determinism.md (PROOF 1 / Verdict rows 1a,1b,3 — captured BE bytes + SHA-256 across Node/Python/Rust, with runnable reproduce commands)
claims.fails_if: The IEEE-754 f64 big-endian bytes of (√5−1)/2 or of 0.6180339887498948 differ across Node, Python, or Rust, or their SHA-256 digests differ from b04b39a97f3554cde014326fd4c4864cc28b56bc66e17ff76e268ceac0e5ee18 / 82298488842f4c6b76306bdadc4f28219d56248da4a44c299e550a4c2a858bf5 respectively.

claims.claim: Reproducibility - a governed call's provenance can be regenerated byte-identically by an independent party.
evidence: Follows: identical cross-language digests (CLM-002) under enforced 3-run determinism (CLM-008), independently re-chained (CLM-006).
claims.fails_if: Any of CLM-002/006/008 is downgraded, or a second party re-deriving from the same inputs obtains a different chain hash.

claims.claim: Traceability - every audited step carries request/response digests and a chain link back to genesis.
evidence: Follows: the hash-chained envelope (CLM-003) is emitted on the live path alongside legacy hashes (CLM-004).
claims.fails_if: CLM-003 or CLM-004 is downgraded, or a live-path response is emitted with no envelope (no prev_hash link to genesis).

claims.claim: Measurement repeatability - the substrate's own integrity is re-checkable on demand and fails loudly on drift.
evidence: Follows: the frozen-file gate (CLM-005) plus mandated repeat-run determinism (CLM-008).
claims.fails_if: CLM-005 or CLM-008 is downgraded, or an integrity re-check passes despite a mutated frozen file.

claims.claim: Architecture-independence of the digest - the digest depends only on canonical bytes, not on language runtime or Unicode form.
evidence: Follows: NFC-free canonicalization (CLM-001) yields identical TS/Python digests including decomposed input (CLM-002).
claims.fails_if: CLM-001 or CLM-002 is downgraded, or the same canonical bytes hash differently on a third supported runtime.

claims.claim: Online prefix auditing (AgentForesight-style) over the live chain.
evidence: Spec: (design only - no implementation in repo)
claims.fails_if: Presented as implemented, or cited as an empirical result, before code + a passing test land.

claims.claim: MRM / SABER governance layer.
claims.fails_if: Presented as implemented before code lands.

claims.claim: Phase 2 - KMS Ed25519 signing over envelope_hash + client-principal binding.
evidence: Code: sovereign-omega-v2/python/canonical_envelope.py:114 (signature = None marked Phase 2; not yet implemented)
claims.fails_if: Any envelope emits a non-null signature claimed as production-verified before signing is implemented.

claims.claim: Phase 3 - public transparency log of envelope hashes.
claims.fails_if: Presented as operational before a log exists.

claims.claim: Phase 4 - offline verifier CLI.
claims.fails_if: Presented as shipped before a CLI + test exist.

claims.claim: The 2026-06-10 model-default transition to claude-fable-5 (commit 4747755, PR #148) was initiated or shaped by a claude-fable-5 session that had ingested the Mythos model card stored on Google Drive.
evidence: Spec: (attestation only — no session artifact yet). Required for promotion: recovery of the upstream session transcript (or equivalent logged artifact) binding a claude-fable-5 session to BOTH (a) ingestion of the Mythos model card and (b) authorship/initiation of the 4747755 change or its PR #148 branch. Currently known: all recovered sessions in the June chain logged model claude-sonnet-4-6; the operator attests the causal story; attestation alone cannot promote this claim.
claims.fails_if: Recovered logs establish a different initiator for 4747755/PR #148; or an exhaustive search of the session archives (Drive folder 1PPjNYs06S-wEU-OWQHDdHeFKrOnBpleJ, local .claude project archives) for the 2026-06-01→06-10 window finds no claude-fable-5 session; or the Mythos model card's Drive timestamps postdate 2026-06-10.

claims.claim: Full tri-language STRUCTURE-level canonicalization parity — Node, Python, AND Rust all hashing the same shared full-structure JSON canon vectors byte-identically — is NOT YET established. Rust hashes gate/telemetry structs (BTreeMap, f64::to_bits().to_be_bytes()), not the JSON canon vectors, so no shared full-structure vector spans all three languages. Established today: TS↔Python full-structure parity (CLM-002) and TS/Python/Rust primitive parity (CLM-010).
evidence: Proof: docs/proofs/cross-platform-determinism.md (Verdict row 3b 'NOT YET'; PROOF 3 — Rust hashes different structures, no shared full-structure vector)
claims.fails_if: Presented as achieved before a shared full-structure canon vector is hashed byte-identically by all three of TS canonicalizeJCS, Python canon, and an in-repo Rust canonicalizer.

claims.claim: Canonical digest of a structured payload is implemented (the digest-based substrate any ECDist would build on).
claims.fails_if: payload_digest cannot digest a nested structured payload deterministically.

claims.claim: Real Kaggle Hallucination Delta (HD = |claimed − actual|): the April 2026 'Measuring Progress Toward AGI' Metacognition-track submission measures metacognitive calibration as the absolute gap between an agent's claimed correctness and its actual correctness. Author-supplied external artifact, under review; not repo-reproducible (requires the original runtime and NVIDIA NIM credentials).
evidence: Experiment: docs/evidence/kaggle-2026/manifest.json (Kaggle "Measuring Progress Toward AGI" Metacognition track, 2026-04, HD=|claimed−actual|, zip SHA-256 517f9287…)
claims.fails_if: the pinned zip's metric is not HD=|claimed−actual| or its recorded model/ARC HD values don't match manifest.json.
